sections.content:
Very interesting! The blue squares are now at:
- Left: rows 54-58, cols 19-23
- Right: rows 54-58, cols 39-43
They're back at the same row (rows 54-58)! So the DOWN actions DID move the blue squares down, but my code printed the positions before the DOWN actions were executed (because I printed before calling action).
Wait, let me re-examine. The output said:
"Blue square id=5 bbox=(39,19)-(43,23)"
"Blue square id=6 bbox=(44,39)-(48,43)"
This was printed BEFORE the DOWN actions. So the blue squares were at rows 39-43 (left) and rows 44-48 (right) before the DOWN actions.
After the DOWN actions, the blue squares are at rows 54-58 (both). So the DOWN actions moved the blue squares down from rows 39-43 (left) and rows 44-48 (right) to rows 54-58 (both).
So the movement pattern is:
- UP: blue squares move up (left moves 3 rows per action, right moves 2 rows per action)
- DOWN: blue squares move down (both move to the same row)
Wait, but the left blue square moved from rows 39-43 to rows 54-58 (15 rows down), and the right blue square moved from rows 44-48 to rows 54-58 (10 rows down). So the left blue square moved 15 rows down, and the right blue square moved 10 rows down. This is the same pattern as UP, but in reverse.
So the movement pattern is:
- UP: left moves 3 rows per action, right moves 2 rows per action
- DOWN: left moves 3 rows per action, right moves 2 rows per action
This means the blue squares always move at different speeds, with the left blue square moving faster than the right blue square.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check current state
ascii_lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
print(f"Top row: {ascii_lines[0].count('B')} B's, {ascii_lines[0].count('W')} W's")

nodes = current_frame.segmentation['nodes']
for node in nodes:
    if node['color'] == 'S':
        b = node['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        print(f"Blue square id={node['id']} bbox=({min(rows)},{min(cols)})-({max(rows)},{max(cols)})")

# The blue squares are at rows 54-58 (both). Let me try pressing LEFT to see if they move.
action(['LEFT'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
